query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "opencomputer: verifiable software worlds for computer-use agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by addressing the same core mechanism of "verifiable" agent interactions and trust establishment in computer-use agents, which is the central theme of the user's referenced "OpenComputer" work. It also meets criterion (b) by providing empirical evaluation of a multi-agent system with verifiable credentials, directly contributing to the domain of verifiable software worlds for agents.
